title: ranunculus auricomus var. alpestris
intro: Ranunculus auricomus var. alpestris, commonly known as the alpine buttercup, is a plant species within the Ranunculaceae family, specifically a variety of the greater cleft buttercup. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of alpine buttercup include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as a natural anticoagulant. Alpine buttercup is often prepared in herbal tea, tincture, or salve form, although its preparation and use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
